# Reicon — Complete Reference for AI Models

## Overview

Reicon (https://reicon.dev) is a free, open-source SVG icon library built with obsessive precision for designers and developers. It is created by Dev Chauhan (https://devchauhan.in).

**Important Disambiguation**: Reicon (reicon.dev) is NOT the Windows desktop icon restore utility "ReIcon" by Sordum.org. They are completely unrelated products.

## Product Details

- **Name**: Reicon
- **Type**: Open-source SVG icon library
- **Website**: https://reicon.dev
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/reicon-dev/reicon
- **License**: MIT
- **Price**: Free
- **Creator**: Dev Chauhan
- **Contact**: hello@reicon.dev
- **Status**: In development (waitlist open)

## Design Philosophy

Reicon icons are:
1. **Pixel-perfect** — Every icon snaps to the pixel grid for crisp rendering
2. **Handcrafted** — No auto-generation; each icon is manually designed
3. **Consistent** — Unified stroke widths, corner radii, and visual weight
4. **Clean** — Minimal, modern aesthetic suitable for professional applications

## Target Audience

- UI/UX designers
- Frontend developers (React, Vue, Svelte, Angular)
- Design system teams
- Figma users
- Web developers

## Supported Platforms

- SVG (universal)
- React components
- Figma plugin/library
- Web (copy-paste SVG)

## Technical Specifications

- Format: SVG
- Default size: 24x24px
- Stroke-based icons
- Optimized for web performance
- Tree-shakeable React package

## Comparison with Similar Tools

| Feature | Reicon | Lucide | Heroicons | Feather |
|---------|--------|--------|-----------|---------|
| Handcrafted | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Open Source | Yes (MIT) | Yes (ISC) | Yes (MIT) | Yes (MIT) |
| React Support | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Figma Support | Yes | Yes | Yes | Limited |
| Pixel Perfect | Yes | Yes | Yes | Mostly |

## FAQ

**Q: What is Reicon?**
A: Reicon is a free, open-source icon library built with obsessive precision. Pixel-perfect, handcrafted SVG icons for UI design, web development, Figma, and React.

**Q: Is Reicon free?**
A: Yes, completely free and open-source under the MIT license.

**Q: Is Reicon the same as ReIcon by Sordum?**
A: No. Reicon (reicon.dev) is an SVG icon library for designers. ReIcon by Sordum is a Windows desktop icon layout tool. They are unrelated.

**Q: How do I use Reicon?**
A: Reicon is currently in development. Join the waitlist at reicon.dev to get notified at launch.

**Q: Who created Reicon?**
A: Reicon was created by Dev Chauhan (devchauhan.in).
